images.caption: Figure 1:Comparison of 2D and 3D guidance in hierarchical VLAs. (a) 2D planners create representational misalignment: unprojecting 2D plans yields flawed 3D guidance, leading to brittle execution. (b) Our 3D-aware planner generates metrically reliable 3D guidance, establishing a shared metric space for robust manipulation.

Figure 2:Overview of3D HAMSTER. The framework decouples semantic planning and motor execution with two-stage training strategy: Stage 1 aligns depth features with the VLM space using a dense reconstruction loss (ℒd​e​p​t​h\mathcal{L}_{depth}) while preserving VLM capabilities; Stage 2 fine-tunes for trajectory prediction. The 3D trajectory planner fuses RGB and depth to generate metrically reliable 3D trajectories, which the trajectory-conditioned 3D low-level policy executes as robust closed-loop actions (𝐚𝐭\mathbf{a_{t}}) from the scene point cloud.

Figure 3:End-to-end manipulation evaluation setups. (a) Simulation environments from the Colosseum benchmark, showcasing various visual and physical perturbations. (b) Real-world setup using a Franka Panda arm equipped with an external RGB-D camera

Figure 4:Qualitative comparison of 3D trajectory predictions. Each trajectory is shown from two viewpoints: baseline predictions that appear plausible in View 1 reveal significant depth errors in View 2, whereas3D HAMSTERremains metrically consistent across both views.

Figure 5:Real-world execution rollouts. HAMSTER’s 2D trajectories cling to the scene surface (top row, graffiti effect), causing failure.3D HAMSTER’s 3D trajectories remain metrically reliable (bottom row), enabling success.
